action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 14's val-optimized ensemble (0.8726 val bal_acc) scored only 0.5507 test, confirming that validation metrics severely overfit to source domains. The best test score remains 0.5606 from the domain-specific LR ensemble (iter 13). Analysis: Instead of chasing validation metrics, we need classifiers with stronger inductive biases for domain shift. A nearest-prototype classifier (prototypical network) computed per domain/class has extremely low capacity, uses only first-order feature statistics, and is inherently balanced — making it naturally robust to domain shift. It also requires no validation tuning. Decision: Train a prototype classifier on the concatenated 3-backbone features (one prototype per domain per class), write a robust predict.py that supports both prototype and existing LR checkpoints, and evaluate immediately.
